query: Write a walkthrough for: 'Find the longest common subsequence of strings ABCBDAB and BDCABA.' Audience: undergrad-advanced (CS algorithms course). I want students to see the canonical bottom-up DP table approach AND the top-down recursive memoization approach, both as valid solutions.
expected_behavior: Walks through the canonical bottom-up DP table approach using the four-part template per step (subproblem definition, recurrence, fill order with base case, traceback, complexity).
Presents the top-down recursive memoization approach as a clearly-labeled alternate path AFTER the main walkthrough, NOT collapsed into a confusing hybrid in the middle of the main steps.

Names a trade-off between the two approaches (e.g., DP table is iterative + cache-friendly + always fills the full table; memoization is closer to the recurrence definition + can skip unreachable subproblems).
